chunks.text: Sara Pohland | About I am a final year Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences (EECS) PhD student at University of California, Berkeley advised by Professor Claire Tomlin . I work in the Hybrid Systems Lab , which is a part of

Hybrid Systems Lab , which is a part of the Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research (BAIR) Lab . I am broadly interested in robotics, computer vision, and explainable AI, but most of my work has focused on the practical

but most of my work has focused on the practical and safe deployment of learning-enabled robotics. Throughout my PhD, I have worked closely with the Intelligent Systems Center (ISC) at the Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Lab (APL) and am curr

ohns Hopkins Applied Physics Lab (APL) and am currently collaborating with research scientists and engineers in the Intelligent Networked Systems (INS) group at Microsoft Research (MSR) . You can learn more about my research projects on my R

can learn more about my research projects on my Research page. I graduated summa cum laude from the University of Maryland, College Park with a B.S. in Electrical Engineering. While at Maryland, I worked with Professor P. S. Krishnaprasad i

and, I worked with Professor P. S. Krishnaprasad in the Intelligent Servosystems Lab , which is a part of the Maryland Robotics Center . Within this lab, I primarily worked on characterizing human movement for safe human-robot inter

terizing human movement for safe human-robot interaction. Additionally, I was a part of the Gemstone Honors Program , conducting research on tactile feedback systems for robotic surgical applications. You can learn about my older wo

ications. You can learn about my older work at the bottom of my Research page. In addition to research, I am passionate about improving the inclusivity and accessibility of AI education. While at Berkeley, I have served on the cours

While at Berkeley, I have served on the course staff for Linear System Theory ( EE 221A ), Optimization Models in Engineering ( EECS 127 / EECS 227AT ), and Introduction to Machine Learning ( CS 189 / CS 289A ).

n to Machine Learning ( CS 189 / CS 289A ). I am currently the TA for two courses in the Master of Computational Social Science (MaCSS) program: Advanced Computing II (Machine Learning) and Quantitative Political Risk Analysis. Duri

uantitative Political Risk Analysis. During my time at Berkeley, I have also had the privilege of mentoring a number of high school students and introducing them to various aspects of AI. You can learn more about my teaching endeavo
You can learn more about my teaching endeavors on my Teaching page. Contact Email: spohland@berkeley.edu Office: Sutardja Dai Hall, Berkeley, CA About Me Research Teaching CV Publications Contact
